segments.text: Lesson 55.

translations.zh-Hans: 第55课。

segments.text: From the Earth Greetings.

translations.zh-Hans: 来自地球的问候。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Which life forms are most likely to develop on a distant planet?

translations.zh-Hans: 哪种生命形式最有可能在遥远的行星上发展起来？

segments.text: Recent developments in astronomy have made it possible to detect planets in our own Milky Way and in other galaxies.

translations.zh-Hans: 天文学的最新进展使我们能够探测银河系及其他星系中的行星。

segments.text: This is a major achievement because, in relative terms, are very small and do not emit light.

translations.zh-Hans: 这是一项重大成就，因为相对而言，行星非常小，而且自身不发光。

segments.text: Finding planets is proving hard enough, but finding life on them will prove infinitely more difficult.

translations.zh-Hans: 寻找行星已经够困难了，而在行星上寻找生命将难上无数倍。

segments.text: The first question to answer is whether a planet can actually support life.

translations.zh-Hans: 首先要回答的问题是，一颗行星是否真的能够维持生命。

segments.text: In our own solar system, for example, Venus is far too hot, and Mars is far too cold to support life.

translations.zh-Hans: 例如，在我们的太阳系中，金星太热，火星又太冷，都无法维持生命。

segments.text: Only the Earth provides ideal conditions, and even here it has taken more than four billion years for plant and animal life to evolve.

translations.zh-Hans: 只有地球具备理想条件；即便在这里，动植物生命也用了四十多亿年才演化出来。

segments.text: Whether a planet can support life depends on the size and brightness of its star, that is, its sun.

translations.zh-Hans: 一颗行星能否维持生命，取决于它所属恒星——也就是它的太阳——的大小和亮度。

segments.text: Imagine a star up to twenty times larger, brighter, and hotter than our own sun.

translations.zh-Hans: 设想有一颗比太阳大、亮、热多达二十倍的恒星。

segments.text: A planet would have to be a very long way from it to be capable of supporting life.

translations.zh-Hans: 一颗行星必须离它非常遥远，才可能维持生命。

segments.text: Alternatively, if the star were small, the life-supporting planet would have to have a close orbit round it and also provide the perfect conditions for life forms to develop.

translations.zh-Hans: 反之，如果恒星较小，能够维持生命的行星就必须在近距离轨道上环绕它，同时还要具备让生命形式发展的完美条件。

segments.text: But how would we find such a planet?

translations.zh-Hans: 但我们怎样才能找到这样的行星呢？

segments.text: At there is no telescope in existence that is capable of detecting the presence of life.

translations.zh-Hans: 目前还不存在能够探测生命存在的望远镜。

segments.text: The development of such a telescope will be one of the great astronomical projects of the twenty-first century.

translations.zh-Hans: 研制这样的望远镜将成为二十一世纪重大的天文学工程之一。

segments.text: It is impossible to look for life on another planet using Earth-based telescopes.

translations.zh-Hans: 使用地面望远镜不可能寻找另一颗行星上的生命。

segments.text: Our own warm atmosphere and the heat generated by the telescope would make it impossible to detect objects as small as planets.

translations.zh-Hans: 地球温暖的大气层以及望远镜自身产生的热量，会使我们无法探测行星那样小的天体。

segments.text: Even a telescope in orbit round the Earth, like the very successful Hubble telescope, would not be suitable because of the dust particles in our solar system.

translations.zh-Hans: 即使是像非常成功的哈勃望远镜那样环绕地球运行的望远镜，也会因太阳系中的尘埃微粒而不适用。

segments.text: A telescope would have to be as far away as the planet Jupiter to look for life in outer space.

translations.zh-Hans: 要寻找外太空的生命，望远镜必须远至木星所在的位置。

segments.text: Because the dust becomes thinner, the further we travel towards the outer edges of our own solar system.

translations.zh-Hans: 这是因为我们越接近太阳系外围，尘埃就越稀薄。

segments.text: Once we detect a planet, we would have to find a way of blotting out the light from its star so that we would be able to see the planet properly and analyze its atmosphere.

translations.zh-Hans: 一旦探测到行星，我们还得设法遮住它所属恒星的光，才能清楚地看到行星并分析其大气。

segments.text: In the first instance, we would be looking for plant life rather than little green men.

translations.zh-Hans: 最初，我们要寻找的是植物，而不是小绿人。

segments.text: The life forms most likely to develop on a planet would be bacteria.

translations.zh-Hans: 最有可能在行星上发展起来的生命形式是细菌。

segments.text: It is bacteria that have generated the oxygen we breathe on Earth.

translations.zh-Hans: 正是细菌产生了我们在地球上呼吸的氧气。

segments.text: For most of the Earth's history, they have been the only form of life on our planet.

translations.zh-Hans: 在地球历史的大部分时间里，细菌一直是地球上唯一的生命形式。

segments.text: As Earth-dwellers, we always cherish the hope that we will be visited by little green men and that we will be able to communicate with them.

translations.zh-Hans: 作为地球居民，我们总盼望小绿人会来拜访，并且我们能够同他们交流。

segments.text: But this hope is always in the realms of science fiction.

translations.zh-Hans: 但这种希望始终只存在于科幻小说的领域。

segments.text: If we were able to discover lowly forms of life like bacteria on another planet, it would completely change our view of ourselves.

translations.zh-Hans: 如果我们能在另一颗行星上发现细菌这样的低等生命形式，就会彻底改变我们对自身的看法。

segments.text: As Daniel Golden of NASA observed, life elsewhere would change everything.

translations.zh-Hans: 正如美国国家航空航天局的丹尼尔·戈尔丁所说，其他地方的生命会改变一切。

segments.text: No human endeavor or thought would be unchanged by it.

translations.zh-Hans: 人类的任何努力和思想都将因此发生变化。
